zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll於1445年11月25日出生在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾時，他的父親Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles33歲，母親Beatrice Douglas16歲。他於1475年之前與Lady Isabel Gordon在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1507年1月14日在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾亨特利享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll于1445年11月25日出生在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾时，他的父亲Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles33岁，母亲Beatrice Douglas16岁。他于1475年之前与Lady Isabel Gordon在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1507年1月14日在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾亨特利享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll was born on 25 November 1445, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, was 33 and his mother, Beatrice Douglas, was 16. He married Lady Isabel Gordon before 1475, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 14 January 1507, in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll est né le 25 novembre 1445, à Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Beatrice Douglas, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Lady Isabel Gordon avant 1475, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé le 14 janvier 1507, à Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll am 25. November 1445 in Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles 33 und seine Mutter Beatrice Douglas 16. Er hat vor 1475 in Scotland Lady Isabel Gordon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 14. Januar 1507 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll è nato il il 25 novembre 1445 nel Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Beatrice Douglas, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Lady Isabel Gordon prima del 1475, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 14 gennaio 1507, nel Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Hay 3rd Earl of Errollが1445年11月25日にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peeblesは33歳，母Beatrice Douglasは16歳であった。1475年以前にスコットランドでLady Isabel Gordonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1507年1月14日にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアハントリーで61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll(이)가 1445년 11월 25일 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice Douglas(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1475년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Lady Isabel Gordon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1507년 1월 14일 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 헌틀리에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.25 November 1445–14 January 1507: 25 de novembro de 1445–14 de janeiro de 1507
pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll nasceu em 25 de novembro de 1445, em Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice Douglas, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Isabel Gordon antes 1475, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 14 de janeiro de 1507, em Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll родился 25 ноября 1445 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Douglas, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Isabel Gordon перед 1475 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер 14 января 1507 г. в местности хантли, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 61 год.

es.25 November 1445–14 January 1507: 25 de noviembre de 1445–14 de enero de 1507
es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll nació el 25 de noviembre de 1445, en Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Beatrice Douglas, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Lady Isabel Gordon antes de 1475, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 14 de enero de 1507, en Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 61 años.
